sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Now:
- White star: at [23, 51] (moved from [14, 57])
- Purple square: at [30, 48] (moved from [25, 51])
- Gray diamond: at [37, 48]
So clicking the purple square moved the white star there, and the purple square moved down toward the gray diamond. The purple square is getting closer to the gray diamond at [37, 48].
So the pattern is:
- Clicking an object moves the white star there
- The clicked object moves along its trail in the direction away from where the white star came from
So to move the purple square toward the purple ring, I need to click objects below it (like the gray diamond), which would push it upward. And to move it downward, I click objects above it.
Wait, that doesn't match. Let me reconsider:
- When I clicked the purple ring at [14, 57], the purple square moved from [32, 47] to [25, 51] (upward toward the ring)
- When I clicked the purple square at [25, 51], it moved to [30, 48] (downward away from the ring)
So clicking an object above the purple square moves it upward, and clicking the purple square itself moves it downward. This suggests the purple square moves in the direction of where the white star was before.
Actually, let me think about it differently. The white star was at [14, 57] (purple ring), and I clicked the purple square at [25, 51]. The purple square moved from [25, 51] to [30, 48], which is downward. The white star moved from [14, 57] to [23, 51] (close to where the purple square was).
So it seems like clicking an object swaps the white star with it, and the object moves in the direction away from the white star's previous position.
To get the purple square to the purple ring, I need to click objects below the purple square, which would push it upward. Let me try clicking the gray diamond at [37, 48].
